candidates.title: Encoder-Driven Feature Absorption: Mechanism and Safety Implications
candidates.summary: Feature absorption in SAEs is driven by the encoder's learned alignment with hierarchical structure, not decoder geometry. The 2x2 factorial confirms encoder sufficiency (B≈D) and decoder irrelevance (C≈A), but with B>D anomaly -- decoder acts as implicit regularizer. Front-runner now incorporates H_Downstream test from contrarian.
hypotheses: H1: Trained SAEs show higher multi-child proportional absorption than random baselines (PASSED)
H_Mech: Absorption is driven by encoder alignment, not decoder geometry (PASSED with B>D anomaly)
H3: Steering absorbed features improves sensitivity (PASSED - 1.62x ratio)
H_Comp: Absorption increases monotonically with hierarchy strength (PASSED pilot)
H_Downstream: Absorbed features show degraded downstream utility (NOT TESTED - NEW)
candidates.pilot_focus: H_Downstream (Priority 0), H_Mech 5-seed stochastic validation, H_Safe on validated features

merge_contributions.pragmatist: Multi-child proportional ablation fixes measurement saturation
merge_contributions.interdisciplinary: Critical branching phase transition for absorption threshold
merge_contributions.contrarian: H_Downstream: does absorption actually matter for downstream utility?
merge_contributions.empiricist: Strict 2x2 factorial design; SynthSAEBench validation required

candidates.drop_trigger: If H_Downstream shows absorption doesn't matter (field optimizing wrong metric)

candidates.title: Safety-Critical Features Are Disproportionately Absorbed
candidates.summary: SAEs may systematically fail to represent rare, safety-critical features due to absorption. NO prior work examines this. Prior placeholder pilot using arbitrary indices is INVALID -- must use Neuronpedia-validated features only.
hypotheses: H_Safe: Safety-critical features have higher absorption rates than matched non-safety features (Mann-Whitney p < 0.05)
candidates.pilot_focus: Gemma Scope SAE (gemma-2b, layer 12), 20 validated safety + 20 matched non-safety features from Neuronpedia
candidates.novelty_note: No prior work examines whether safety-critical features are systematically more absorbed. Genuinely novel (9/10).
candidates.drop_trigger: If safety-relevant features do not show elevated absorption rates on real SAEs (p > 0.3)

candidates.title: Encoder Regularization to Reduce Absorption
candidates.summary: Based on B>D anomaly, encoder-only regularization may reduce absorption by leveraging decoder's implicit regularizing effect. OrtSAE modifies both encoder+decoder; our approach targets encoder only as cleaner intervention.
hypotheses: H_EncReg: Encoder regularization penalizing parent-child activation correlation reduces absorption >30% with <5% reconstruction degradation
candidates.pilot_focus: Implement encoder-only orthogonality penalty; compare vs OrtSAE
candidates.novelty_note: First encoder-targeted intervention for absorption reduction (prior work modifies both encoder and decoder).
candidates.drop_trigger: If regularization degrades reconstruction >10% or reduces absorption <10%

candidates.title: Training-Free Absorption Proxy Validation
candidates.summary: Validate encoder-decoder asymmetry as training-free absorption predictor against SynthSAEBench ground truth. The field needs training-free detection for deep layers where ablation fails.
hypotheses: H_Proxy: Encoder-decoder asymmetry predicts absorption with AUC > 0.75 against ablation ground truth
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute asymmetry metrics on SynthSAEBench; evaluate AUC-ROC
candidates.novelty_note: First systematic validation of training-free proxy metrics against ground truth.
candidates.drop_trigger: If no proxy achieves AUC > 0.70 against ground truth

candidates.title: Multi-Resolution SAE Ensemble for Hierarchical Feature Recovery
candidates.summary: Train ensemble of SAEs with varying L0 targets (16, 64, 256) to collectively recover hierarchical features. WEAK BACKUP due to overlap with Gadgil et al. and high cost.
hypotheses: H_Ens: High-L0 SAE captures child features that low-L0 SAE has absorbed
candidates.pilot_focus: DEFERRED -- requires clear differentiation from Gadgil et al.
candidates.novelty_note: Overlaps with Gadgil et al. on ensemble concept. L0 diversity mechanism is differentiator but novelty only 5/10.

pool_status.dropped_notes: cand_geom dropped because H_Mech shows decoder geometry contributes nothing (original proposal was decoder-based). cand_eco dropped as reframing only (Barlow 1961 is classical).

novelty_assessment.cand_safe: 9/10 - No prior work on safety-critical feature absorption
novelty_assessment.cand_p1: 7/10 - Novel factorial decomposition methodology + B>D anomaly
novelty_assessment.cand_encreg: 7/10 - Novel encoder-only intervention (vs both in OrtSAE)
novelty_assessment.cand_ens: 5/10 - Partial overlap with Gadgil et al.

critical_findings_from_prior: B>D ANOMALY: trained encoder + random decoder produces MORE absorption than both trained
H1: trained SAE absorption significantly higher than random (delta=0.353)
H3: absorbed features MORE sensitive to steering (1.62x ratio)
H2: positive frequency correlation (rho=+0.171) contradicts competitive exclusion, supports efficient coding
lessons_incorporated: Remove placeholder safety features (arbitrary indices) -- use Neuronpedia validation only
Fix sensitivity formula bug before H_Pareto claims
H_Downstream added to directly test contrarian's core challenge
B>D anomaly now central finding, not footnote
